title: Yirt-4138-Grek-12

{{Quote|Yirt-4138-Grek-12, a 65-kilometer diameter airless rock, became the grave-site for 25 cybernetically-enhanced data thieves when Corporate Alliance tank droids leveled a clandestine compound funded by unknown parties.|HoloNet News report|<ref name="HNN" />}}
'''Yirt-4138-Grek-12''' was a [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]] [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] located in the [[Fakir sector/Legends|Fakir sector]]. It was the site of a [[Yirt-4138-Grek-12 slicer compound|pressurized compound]] where [[Cyborg slicers of Yirt-4138-Grek-12|slicers]] were outfitted with [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetic]] implants and trained in industrial [[Spy/Legends|espionage]] targeting the [[Corporate Alliance/Legends|Corporate Alliance]]. After Alliance [[expeditor]]s traced [[Denlace Sachoore|one]] such data [[Thief/Legends|thief's]] [[Doctor/Legends|surgery]] and training back to the compound, the organization [[Raid on Yirt-4138-Grek-12|dispatched]] [[NR-N99 Persuader-class droid enforcer/Legends|NR-N99 ''Persuader''-class droid enforcers]] to the asteroid. The [[Droid tank/Legends|droid tanks]] ultimately destroyed the facility, [[Death/Legends|killing]] twenty-five slicers present at the [[Time/Legends|time]].

==Description==
{{Quote|Forces of the Corporate Alliance unleashed volleys of missile-delivered retribution to an industrial espionage training center on a remote asteroid in the Fakir sector.|HoloNet News report|<ref name="HNN" />}}
Yirt-4138-Grek-12 was a remote [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]]<ref name="HNN" /> located in the [[Yirt-4138-Grek-12 system/Legends|Yirt-4138-Grek-12 system]] of the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies']] [[Fakir sector/Legends|Fakir sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It was an [[Atmosphere/Legends|airless]] rock sixty-five [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] in diameter, and its designation included<ref name="HNN" /> the [[Aurebesh/Legends|Aurebesh]] [[Writing/Legends|letters]] [[Yirt/Legends|yirt]] and [[Grek/Legends|grek]].<ref name="CSWE Aurebesh">{{CSWECite|I|48|Aurebesh}}</ref>

==History==
{{Quote|Corporate Alliance transports delivered the tank droids 10 kilometers from the compound, which then traveled overland and breached the structure's pressurization with missile and laser volleys.|HoloNet News report|<ref name="HNN" />}}
Yirt-4138-Grek-12, which was situated in [[Corporate Alliance/Legends|Corporate Alliance]] [[Realspace/Legends|space]], was the site of a [[Yirt-4138-Grek-12 slicer compound|compound]],<ref name="HNN" /> believed to have been funded by [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]],<ref name="UA">''[[Ultimate Adversaries]]''</ref> where [[Cyborg slicers of Yirt-4138-Grek-12|data thieves]] were outfitted with [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetic]] implants and trained as industrial [[Spy/Legends|spies]] targeting the Corporate Alliance. After the Alliance [[Clerk/Legends|clerk]] [[Denlace Sachoore]] was arrested for embezzling data from the organization, Alliance [[expeditor]]s traced her [[Doctor/Legends|surgery]] and training back to the [[Slicer/Legends|slicer]] compound.<ref name="HNN" />

[[File:TankDroidHolonet.png|thumb|right|295px|The Corporate Alliance used NR-N99 ''Persuader''-class droid enforcers (pictured) to destroy a slicer compound on Yirt-4138-Grek-12.]]
Corporate Alliance [[Transport/Legends|transports]] subsequently [[Raid on Yirt-4138-Grek-12|delivered]] [[NR-N99 Persuader-class droid enforcer/Legends|NR-N99 ''Persuader''-class droid enforcers]] to a point ten kilometers from the compound. The [[Droid tank/Legends|tank droids]] then approached the facility overland and,<ref name="HNN" /> making no warning or effort to take [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]],<ref name="FF91">{{FactFile|91|COR 1-2|Corporate Alliance}}</ref> opened fire. Using volleys of [[Missile/Legends|missiles]] and [[Laser/Legends|laser]] shots, they breached the compound's pressurization and ultimately leveled the structure, [[Death/Legends|killing]] twenty-five slicers present at the [[Time/Legends|time]].<ref name="HNN" />

On the [[Great ReSynchronization|date]] [[22 BBY/Legends|13:3:21]], the raid on the Yirt-4138-Grek-12 compound was reported on from the [[Fakir Sector Node]] of the [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]] for an issue of the [[HoloNet News CoCo District Edition|CoCo District Edition]] of the [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]]. The report included a comment by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Gopple]], who, although condemning the Corporate Alliance's raid as excessive, nevertheless admitted that, due to the asteroid's location in the Alliance's space and jurisdiction, the organization's actions complied with existing legislation.<ref name="HNN" />

==Inhabitants==
{{Quote|A rash of corporate thievery was discovered in the Fakir sector last month with the arrest of Denlace Sachoore, a 24-year old Corporate Alliance clerk found embezzling data in a cranium-implanted vessel drive. Questioning by Alliance expeditors traced her surgery and training back to the asteroid compound.|HoloNet News report|<ref name="HNN" />}}
At the time of the destruction of the Yirt-4138-Grek-12 slicer compound, twenty-five [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] data [[Thief/Legends|thieves]] were present there.<ref name="HNN" />

==Locations==
{{Quote|The compound contained unauthorized cybernetic surgery suites that were churning out vessels and cyborgs that have been slicing into Corporate Alliance data-stores.|Corporate Alliance [[Magistrate/Legends|Magistrate]] [[Passel Argente/Legends|Passel Argente]], in a HoloNet News report|<ref name="HNN" />}}
A pressurized slicer compound was located on Yirt-4138-Grek-12. It featured unauthorized cybernetic surgery suites and served as an industrial espionage training center.<ref name="HNN" />

==Behind the scenes==
Yirt-4138-Grek-12 was introduced in [[HoloNet News Vol. 531 48|''HoloNet News Vol. 531'' #48]], a representation of an [[in-universe]] issue of the HoloNet News<ref name="HNN" /> written by [[Pablo Hidalgo]] and [[Paul Ens]]<ref name="Credits">{{HoloNetNewsWeb||credits.html|Credits}}</ref> and published on the [[HoloNet News (original website)|HoloNet News website]] on [[March 21]], [[2002]].<ref name="HNN48 date">{{JCFCite|url=posts/6128630|board=Literature|thread=The Official HoloNetNews Discussion Thread|user=PrinceXizor|date=2002-03-21|time=3:24 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20220914072656/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-official-holonetnews-discussion-thread.5990764/page-6#post-6128630}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Yirt-4138-Grek-12 [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the asteroid itself, in grid square K-9.<ref name="TEA" /> The [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 118|118th issue]] of [[De Agostini]]'s ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]'' magazine,<ref name="FF118">{{FactFile|118|COR 1-2|Corporate Alliance Tank Droid}}</ref> which was written by [[Iain Lowson]]<ref name="FF Author">{{LinkedIn|url=in/iainlowson|text=Iain Lowson|archivefile=File:IainLowson-LinkedIn.png}}</ref> and published around [[March 31]], [[2004]],<ref name="FF118Date">{{FFdates|118}}</ref> erroneously claimed that the Corporate Alliance raid on Yirt-4138-Grek-12 instead took place at the Fakir Sector Node.<ref name="FF118" /> The error was later repeated in the [[The Official Star Wars Fact File Part 26|twenty-sixth issue]] of the magazine's [[2014]] [[The Official Star Wars Fact File (2014)|re-make edition]],<ref name="FF26">{{FactFile2014|26|COR 1-2|Corporate Alliance Tank Droid}}</ref> also written by Lowson,<ref name="FF Author" /> with that issue being released [[July 2]], 2014.<ref name="FF26Date">{{FactFile2014Dates|26}}</ref>
